definitions.definition: The definite integral of a function f(x) from a to b is denoted by ∫[a, b] f(x) dx and represents the area under the curve of f(x) between the limits a and b.
The indefinite integral of a function f(x) is denoted by ∫f(x) dx and represents a family of functions, one of which is the antiderivative of f(x).
The function for which the integral is to be evaluated is called the integrand.
The lower limit of integration is the lower bound of the interval over which the integral is evaluated.
The upper limit of integration is the upper bound of the interval over which the integral is evaluated.
The area under a curve of a function f(x) between two points a and b is given by the definite integral ∫[a, b] f(x) dx.
An improper integral is an integral that has infinite limits of integration or the integrand becomes infinite at some point within the interval of integration.
The integral of a power function f(x) = x^n is given by ∫x^n dx = (x^(n+1))/(n+1) + C.
The integral of a constant multiple of a function f(x) is given by ∫k*f(x) dx = k*∫f(x) dx.

definitions.term: Integral of a Sum or Difference
definitions.definition: The integral of a sum or difference of two functions f(x) and g(x) is given by ∫[f(x) ± g(x)] dx = ∫f(x) dx ± ∫g(x) dx.
The fundamental theorem of calculus states that differentiation and integration are inverse processes.
The function of a definite integral is a composite function that involves a definite integral as a function.
Integration by parts is a method used to evaluate the integral of a product of two functions f(x) and g(x).
Integration by partial fractions is a method used to evaluate the integral of a rational function.
Integration by substitution is a method used to evaluate the integral of a function by substituting a new variable.
A function f(x) is said to be integrable if its definite integral exists.
